name: Allen Brain Atlas - Human Brain Gene Expression and Cell Types
version: ABC Atlas (Allen Brain Cell Atlas) - Current as of 2026

spatial_transcriptomics_data.description: Largest cell-resolution spatial transcriptomics dataset in human brain

multiomics_data.description: Gene expression and regulatory state measured in same cells

brain_coverage.developmental_coverage_future: Fetal and early postnatal in development

regulatory_relevance_explanation: Allen Brain Atlas provides cell-type expression context for understanding where and in which cells regulatory variants might have functional impact. Does not directly annotate regulatory elements (that is ENCODE's role), but essential for: (1) determining which cell types express autism genes, (2) identifying cell-type-specific vulnerability to regulatory variants, (3) connecting variants to cell-type-specific consequences.

autism_relevance.excitatory_inhibitory_balance: Enables exploration of E/I imbalance hypothesis through detailed GABAergic and glutamatergic neuron profiling
autism_relevance.receptor_type_separation: Supports identification of glutamate-specific vs. GABA-specific effects through neuron subtype specificity
autism_relevance.cell_vulnerability: Can identify which cell types express multiple autism genes (vulnerable types)
autism_relevance.network_analysis: Enables cell-type-specific network analysis (TF→gene connections within cell types)

publication_key.title: A brain cell atlas integrating single-cell transcriptomes across human brain regions

related_publications.title: A multimodal cell census and atlas of the mammalian primary motor cortex
related_publications.description: Similar methodology and cell-type taxonomy approach; foundation for ABC Atlas

download_methods: Interactive web portal (no download needed for browsing)
Local analysis via standard single-cell tools (Seurat, Scanpy)

integration_plan.phase_1: Download and parse ABC Atlas cell-type taxonomy and gene expression
integration_plan.phase_2: Map autism candidate genes to cell-type expression profiles
integration_plan.phase_3: Integrate with ENCODE/GTEx to connect variants→regulatory elements→cell types
integration_plan.phase_4: Build cell-type-specific prediction models for noncoding variant effects

atlas_outputs: Annotated table: autism genes with cell-type expression profiles
Cell-type expression specificity scores for each autism gene
technical_challenges: Computational resources needed for expression analysis
Limited coverage of some brain regions (MTG + DFC currently comprehensive)
developmental_limitation: Currently adult brain only. Must combine with BrainSpan for developmental trajectory. Developing mouse brain atlas methods being extended to humans.
validation_strategies: Enrichment analysis (autism genes in specific cell types)
